Open a fresh Colab notebook or Python environment with TensorFlow 2.12 installed
Clone or download the TensorFlow Models repository from https://github.com/tensorflow/models
Navigate to the 'docs/vision' directory inside the cloned repository
Run all notebook cells sequentially up to and including the cell titled 'Import required libraries from tensorflow models as image below'
Specifically, in the import cell, attempt to run: 'from official.vision import ...' or other imports referencing the 'official' module
Observe whether the import statement raises an ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError related to 'official' or its submodules
Capture and log the exact error message and traceback displayed upon the failed import
Verify that TensorFlow 2.12 is installed by running 'import tensorflow as tf; print(tf.__version__)' before the import cell
Check if the 'official' package from TensorFlow Model Garden is installed or accessible in the environment by running 'pip show tf-models-official' or attempting 'import official'
If 'official' is not installed, attempt to install it via 'pip install tf-models-official' and rerun the import cell to confirm the error reproduces without this installation
Create an assertion that confirms the ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError is triggered exactly at the import line from 'official.vision' in the notebook
